Document:
# Two-Handed Melee
[textlink="View Full Wiki Page" link="https://ss14.misfitsystems.net/wiki/index.php/Two-Handed%20Melee"/]
## Two-Handed Melee
Two-handed melee weapons are slower to swing but deal significantly more damage. They require both hands to use, meaning you cannot hold a firearm at the same time - but in melee range, their power is unmatched.
Spears are versatile two-handed weapons with reach. They can be thrown and deal good damage against armored and unarmored targets alike.
[bold]Weapon[/bold] [bold]Notes[/bold] [bold]Wasteland Spear[/bold] Basic reaching weapon. Can be thrown. [bold]Tribal Spear[/bold] Decorated tribal construction. Similar stats to the Wasteland Spear. [bold]Flag Spear[/bold] Spear with faction banner. Ceremonial but fully combat-capable.
Blunt force weapons that crush through armor. The Super Sledge is the pinnacle of two-handed blunt weaponry.
[bold]Weapon[/bold] [bold]Notes[/bold] [bold]Sledgehammer[/bold] Heavy blunt weapon. Good damage against armored targets. [bold]Super Sledge[/bold] Pre-war hydraulic sledge. Exceptional damage. Rare and prized. [bold]Tribal War Club[/bold] Large blunt club. Slower swing, heavy impact. [bold]Nail Board[/bold] Improvised spiked club. Common Wasteland weapon.
Large swords and blades for those who prefer cutting over crushing. Many are tribal or ceremonial in origin.
[bold]Weapon[/bold] [bold]Notes[/bold] [bold]Longsword[/bold] Standard two-handed blade. Balanced reach and damage. [bold]Tribal Sword[/bold] Handcrafted long blade. Rougher edge than manufactured swords. [bold]Tribal Machete[/bold] Oversized two-handed cutting blade. [bold]Ripper[/bold] Motorized blade weapon. Pre-war construction. Very high damage.
